summarization.a: The chart shows the mass gain over time at different temperatures (27°C, 77°C, 127°C, 177°C). The mass gain increases stepwise with time, indicating a cumulative effect, characteristic of ALD.

a.question: What is the duration of a single cycle and how can this be concluded from the graph?
a.answer: a	The duration of single cycle is 1.6 seconds. This can be concluded from the graph that there are 10 cycles in a time span of 16 seconds in the figure.

a.question: Is there cyclic growth for the process at 27 degrees?
a.answer: a	No there is no cyclic growth. For ALD the increase every cycle should be the same, this is not the case fpr 27 degrees Celcius.

a.question: For which temperatures do the GPCs overlap?
Why is a processing temperature of 77 degrees not preferred when working with water as one of the reactants?
a.answer: a	Water can condensate below a 100 degrees Celcius, so a process temperature of 77 degrees can result in liquid water in the reactor.
